Title: Gold recoups losses as oil prices fall on Iran deal
Article:
BENGALURU: Gold rose more than one per cent on Thursday, recouping losses from the previous session, as oil prices fell after the US and Iran signed an interim agreement to end their war.
FUNDAMENTALS
Spot gold rose 1.5 per cent to US$4,322.41 per ounce by 0102 GMT after declining 1.7 per cent on Wednesday. US gold futures for August delivery fell 0.9 per cent to US$4,343.10.
The United States and Iran released the text of their interim agreement on Wednesday, with US President Donald Trump threatening to resume attacks and kill Iranian officials if they failed to honour their commitments.
The 14-point agreement extends the ceasefire announced in April by another 60 days to allow the two sides to negotiate a final truce.
Oil prices fell, reversing gains made on Wednesday after Trump said he could resume his bombing campaign if Iran's leaders "don't behave".
The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady on Wednesday, but policymakers expect a rate hike later this year amid growing concerns about inflation remaining above the US central bank's two per cent target.
Nine of the central bank's 19 policymakers now believe they will need to raise the policy rate this year, according to projections published on Wednesday.
Major stock indexes fell, bond yields rose and the US dollar extended gains on Wednesday after the Fed projections showed officials expect higher borrowing costs later this year amid increasing inflation concerns.
Spot silver rose 2.2 per cent to US$69.51 per ounce, platinum gained 1.8 per cent to US$1,767.53, and palladium was up two per cent at US$1,338.67.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor BN set to unveil candidate line-up on Saturday, says Onn Hafiz
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor BN is expected to finalise its candidate list for the state election by the end of this week, with an announcement likely to be made on Saturday.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the candidate selection process for the 16th Johor state election is 80 per cent complete, although the final line-up remained subject to changes pending approval from the coalition's top leadership.
"The candidate selection process has entered its final stage, but no decision should be regarded as final until official appointment letters are issued.
"Everything can still change. Nothing is cast in stone until the day you receive your watikah (appointment letter). There have been instances where people received their watikah and it was later withdrawn," he said during an interview with selected Johor media members at the Menteri Besar's official residence in Saujana yesterday.
The caretaker menteri besar said candidate selection is not a decision made solely at the state level, noting that all names must receive the endorsement of BN chairman and Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, as well as the coalition's component party leadership.
Onn Hafiz, who is also the incumbent Machap assemblyman, said BN's candidate selection criteria centred on service, acceptability and local familiarity rather than age.
"A candidate must be a local, known in the constituency and accepted by the community. Most importantly, the candidate must have a strong service track record and be winnable.
"For me, whether a person is young or old is not the main factor. The candidate must be able to serve the people. There is no point having a young and energetic candidate if he cannot serve the rakyat effectively," he said.
Onn Hafiz said BN's final line-up would likely comprise a mix of experienced and younger candidates from diverse professional backgrounds, reflecting the coalition's commitment to fielding the strongest candidates in every constituency.
Meanwhile, he described young and first-time voters as a crucial demographic that could influence the outcome of the election and the formation of the next state government.
He said that voters below the age of 40 account for between 20 and 40 per cent of Johor's population, making engagement with younger constituents increasingly important.
"Most of our elected representatives have already engaged extensively with young voters and understand their expectations and concerns," he said.
Onn Hafiz also called on Johoreans residing and working outside the state, particularly those employed in Singapore, to return home and fulfil their democratic responsibility on polling day.
He urged party members who may not be selected as candidates to remain loyal to BN and continue serving the people.
"The struggle is bigger than any individual. Those who are not chosen should remain steadfast and continue putting the rakyat first," he said.
Johor goes to the polls on July 11, with nomination day set for June 27 and early voting scheduled for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dollar clings to two-month peak as Fed rate-hike bets mount
Article:
HONG KONG: The US dollar clung to a more than two-month high on Thursday as markets ramped up wagers on Federal Reserve rate hikes this year, heaping fresh pressure on the Japanese yen towards intervention territory.
The US central bank held rates steady in a 3.50 per cent-3.75 per cent range as new chair Kevin Warsh opened a new era with a sweeping policy review. Nearly half of policymakers, however, now expect a rate hike this year amid mounting inflation concerns.
The Fed funds futures market has now priced in an 83 per cent chance of Federal Reserve tightening in December, according to CME FedWatch, with a strong retail sales reading further adding to hawkish bets.
Fresh Gulf uncertainties continued to sap risk appetite after US President Donald Trump said he could resume attacks if Iran violates the ceasefire agreement, keeping oil prices elevated and the dollar supported. Iran's leaders did not address the new threats.
The euro last traded slightly higher at US$1.1511 and sterling strengthened to US$1.3318 after both currencies touched two-month lows earlier.
The risk-sensitive Australian and New Zealand dollars were both up about 0.2 per cent at US$0.7025 and US$0.5780, respectively.
The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, was little changed at 100.31.
It surged 0.85 per cent to its strongest level since March 31 in the previous session, marking its biggest single-day gain since March 2.
"The dollar is up making some sizeable gains... this is going to take a little while to shrug off," NAB senior markets strategist Gavin Friend said in a podcast. "It looks like we could be pushing into new territory here for the dollar."
The Japanese yen weakened to as much as 160.760 after hitting its weakest level since 2024 overnight, continuing to hover around the 160 level widely seen as a line in the sand for potential official intervention.
Elsewhere, the Bank of England appears on course to keep interest rates unchanged at 3.75 per cent later on Thursday as it assesses what a tentative truce in the Iran war means for inflation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Statutory body CEO to face seven graft charges over RM1mil sports facility bribes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A chief executive officer of a statutory body will face seven corruption charges at the Sessions Court today for allegedly soliciting and accepting bribes amounting to more than RM1 million.
The suspect, in his 40s, is expected to be charged in connection with a multi-million-ringgit sports facility upgrading project.
Sources said the alleged offences were linked to the awarding of a tender for upgrading works at a national sports facility and were believed to have been committed on several occasions earlier this year.
He is expected to be produced before Sessions Court judge Suzana Hussin.
The suspect was previously arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) following an investigation into allegations that he had solicited and received money from a contractor as an inducement related to the project.
He was arrested at a shopping mall in Bukit Jalil shortly after allegedly receiving RM100,000 in cash from a contractor.
Preliminary investigations revealed that he had allegedly demanded RM1 million as an inducement to approve payment claims related to the project.
The MACC believed that he had earlier received RM500,000 in cash before Aidilfitri as an advance payment, followed by another RM100,000 from the contractor during the transaction that led to his arrest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTViral: 'Waterfall view' in property listing near TTDI raises concerns [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A property sales pitch near Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) has drawn concern after a real estate agent advertised a unit with a "waterfall view" that appeared to be heavy rainwater runoff from a slope.
The post, shared on Threads by user @hanahdbee, included a video showing water cascading down the slope during a downpour.
Users said the flow did not resemble a natural waterfall, noting there was no visible river source and that the water appeared to flow into a drain below.
According to the post, the unit is located about eight minutes from TTDI.
"I went to see one of the owner's unit yesterday during heavy rain. His living hall can see a waterfall," the user wrote.
She said the owner was selling the 1,200 sq ft unit for RM860,000, with four bedrooms and two bathrooms.
View on Threads
Several users said such heavy runoff during a downpour should not be marketed casually, as it could point to possible drainage or slope stability issues.
User thearties.on.the.go suggested the sight could affect the property's value.
"Probably the price will be reduced soon? That's an additional risk added," the user wrote.
Another user, mk_hze, warned that the water flow should be treated seriously.
"That maybe is not a waterfall. That is a sign of a flash flood or landslide coming soon," the user wrote.
The New Straits Times has contacted Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer to join IPO-bound OpenAI
Article:
BENGALURU: Noam Shazeer, a vice president of engineering at Google and co-lead of its Gemini artificial intelligence models, said on Wednesday that he will leave the company to join IPO-bound OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT.
Shazeer's move comes as leading AI firms compete for talent while racing to develop advanced models.
The surprise departure comes less than two years after Google reportedly paid US$2.7 billion to bring Shazeer, the former head of Character AI, back to the company along with a team of his researchers.
In 2024, Google appointed Shazeer to co-lead the development of its Gemini AI model, and he has been credited as a key figure behind Gemini's ability to close the gap on OpenAI's ChatGPT.
The timing of Shazeer's departure from Google was not immediately clear.
"I'm incredibly proud of the amazing team at Google and everything we've built together," Shazeer said on X, adding that he was "excited" to join OpenAI.
Google said in a statement to Reuters that it was grateful for Shazeer's meaningful contributions to the company over the years.
Shazeer joined Google in 2000 and was a co-author of a seminal 2017 research paper that catalysed the AI boom.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea to apply zero tariffs on LNG and LPG
Article:
SEJONG: South Korea will apply zero tariff rates on liquefied natural gas (LNG) and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) imports within quota limits in the second half of 2026 as it seeks to ease inflationary pressures amid continued volatility in global energy prices.
The Ministry of Economy and Finance said tariffs on LNG, LPG and crude oil used in LPG production will be reduced to zero from July 1, replacing an earlier plan to lower LNG tariffs to two per cent in the third quarter and one per cent in the fourth quarter, while cutting LPG-related tariffs to one per cent in the second half.
The government said the move is expected to help stabilise consumer prices by lowering utility and transportation costs, as inflation rose 3.1 per cent year-on-year in May, the fastest pace in 26 months.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu's fate in PN down to supreme council majority vote
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu's future in Perikatan Nasional (PN) will be decided by a majority vote in the PN supreme council meeting.
PN Youth chief Afnan Hamimi Taib Azamudden said if the supreme council fails to decide on Bersatu's status, the matter will be referred to the Presidential Council for a consensus.
"If the supreme council can make a decision, then it does not need to be referred to the Presidential Council. So, whether they stay or not depends on the majority decision of the supreme council meeting," he said.
He was responding to statements by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, who said the party had decided to remain in PN and would contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections using the coalition's logo.
Muhyiddin, who is the Pagoh member of parliament, had said that as a founding member of PN, there was no reason for Bersatu to leave the coalition.
He also said that Bersatu was prepared to face Pas in the Johor and Negri Sembilan
state elections
if negotiations to repair ties between the two parties failed.
Commenting on this, Afnan Hamimi, who is the Alor Star member of parliament, said Pas was unfazed by Bersatu's open declaration of its readiness to face the party.
The Pas Youth chief claimed that even when the two parties were in a political alliance, Bersatu had already challenged the spirit of camaraderie.
"On the issue of them wanting to fight Pas, even before this, when Pas still had ties with them, they had already fought and challenged the camaraderie of Pas.
"Pas will continue to fight DAP and Pakatan Harapan (PH). Pas is committed to the agenda of uniting the ummah. Any party that fights Pas can be considered as helping DAP and PH," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HSBC Australia faces US$24.6mil penalty over scam protection failures
Article:
BENGALURU: HSBC's Australian unit has admitted to serious failures in protecting customers from scams and could face a A$35 million (US$24.59 million) penalty, subject to court approval, Australia's corporate regulator said on Thursday.
The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) and HSBC will jointly seek approval from the Federal Court for the proposed penalty.
ASIC said the case is among the first globally to target a bank's handling of scam risks and customer protection failures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dentist, disabled entrepreneur put purpose beyond profit
Article:
Zahratulhayat Mat Arif
Your browser does not support the audio element.
IPOH: For Dr Farah Shazwani Mohamd Tarmizi, her work as a dentist and non-governmental organisation leader is part of a single purpose shaped since childhood.
That sense of service, she said, began at the age of 9 when she would accompany her father, Mohamad Tarmizi Abdul Hamid, to religious talks and community programmes.
"I grew up watching my father deliver lectures. That was how I was exposed to audiences at a very young age."
That early foundation evolved into a dual commitment.
The 43-year-old is a specialist in restorative dentistry at the Kampung Simee Dental Clinic.
She also leads Salwa Perak, an NGO that works with single mothers and asnaf groups.
Dr Farah said the Siti Khadijah Award (Women Personality) she received at the state Maal Hijrah celebration is the result of the team supporting her work.
Meanwhile, disabled entrepreneur Khairuddin Mazli, who received the Asnaf Icon Award, has built a reputation for giving back to society through his massage and reflexology business.
The 35-year-old said he has been channelling some of his earnings towards paying surau utility bills and helping orphans in Meru Raya for more than two years.
He has also branched his business into training, allowing others to gain skills in massage and reflexology.
Khairuddin hopes to teach more people like him, so that they can achieve financial independence.
"I want to bring other persons with disabilities into this field so we can grow together," said Khairuddin, who has been running the business for 11 years.
Both received their awards from Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah at a hotel here on Tuesday night. They each took home RM5,000, a plaque and a certificate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil slips again as US, Iran sign peace deal
Article:
BEIJING: Oil prices fell in early trading on Thursday after the US and Iran signed an interim agreement to end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and waive US sanctions on Tehran's oil exports, resolving the largest energy supply disruption in history.
Brent crude futures fell 89 cents, or 1.12 per cent, to US$78.66 a barrel as of 0005 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude declined 98 cents, or 1.28 per cent, to US$75.81 a barrel.
The benchmarks resumed their decline, reversing gains made on Wednesday after US President Donald Trump said he could resume his bombing campaign if Iran's leaders "don't behave".
"The sell-off extended as energy markets continued to aggressively price in a faster-than-expected return of Iranian barrels following the recent US-Iran memorandum of understanding," IG market analyst Tony Sycamore said in a note.
The 14-point memorandum begins a 60-day negotiation period during which Iran will allow toll-free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas shipping lane. The deal calls for traffic through the strait to be restored to full capacity within 30 days.
The preliminary accord defers many of the more difficult issues, including Iran's nuclear programme, and also requires the US and its partners to develop a US$300 billion financing plan to support Iran's recovery.
If the agreement is successfully implemented and the strait is reopened, this year's supply crisis could turn into a significant supply glut in 2027, the International Energy Agency (IEA) cautioned on Wednesday. In its monthly market report, the agency forecast that supply would exceed demand by 5.05 million barrels per day next year as Middle East oil returns to the market.
The US Federal Reserve is also increasingly weighing whether it will need to raise interest rates later this year to rein in inflation, which could slow economic growth and dampen oil demand.
Projections released on Wednesday showed that nine of 19 Federal Reserve policymakers now expect a rate hike to be needed, a departure from three months ago when none held that view.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan's Nikkei gauge crosses 71,000 on US-Iran peace deal
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's Nikkei share gauge surged past the 71,000 level for the first time on Thursday as a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran boosted demand for risk assets.
The benchmark Nikkei 225 jumped 1.88 per cent to 71,219.45, after touching an intraday high of 71,293.64. The broader Topix gained 1.67 per cent to 4,080.26.
The US and Iran released the text of an interim agreement to end their war, with US President Donald Trump threatening to resume attacks and kill Iranian officials if they failed to honour their commitments.
Markets in Asia also reacted to a hawkish tilt by the US Federal Reserve, which held interest rates steady. The dollar strengthened broadly, while the yen touched a near two-year low, approaching levels that prompted Tokyo to intervene in markets.
Chip-related shares led the Nikkei's gains, with Socionext jumping 9.19 per cent and Screen Holdings rising 4.96 per cent.
Financial stocks also advanced, with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group up 2.44 per cent.
Marine transportation stocks lagged, with Nippon Yusen falling 2.08 per cent.
Of the Nikkei's 225 stocks, 144 advanced while 75 declined.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia-Turkmenistan ties set for boost as Anwar visits Ashgabat
Article:
ASHGABAT (Turkmenistan): Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's June 18 to 19 visit to Turkmenistan is expected to boost bilateral cooperation, particularly in oil and gas, as well as sectors such as transportation, logistics and agriculture.
Malaysia's Ambassador to Turkmenistan, Mohd Suhaimi Ahmad Tajuddin, said the visit would also pave the way for greater collaboration in trade and investment, higher education and tourism, while creating opportunities for Malaysian companies to expand exports, especially in halal products and palm oil.
However, he said one of the most important outcomes would involve national oil company Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), which is expected to continue its operations in the Central Asian nation, one of the world's largest oil and gas producers, for another 20 to 25 years through an agreement expected to be signed during the visit.
Suhaimi said the visit, undertaken at the invitation of Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov, also reciprocates the president's official visit to Malaysia on Dec 18-19, 2024.
Petronas has been operating in Turkmenistan, known as the "White Marble" country, since 1996 and has invested US$11.8 billion there.
Last year, Petronas signed a new agreement with the Turkmen government and a new partner, XRG, an international investment subsidiary of Abu Dhabi National Oil Company.
"The agreement enables them to continue operating in Turkmenistan for another 20 to 25 years. New opportunities are expected to be discussed by the prime minister and the President of Turkmenistan, and the outcomes are expected to be announced by the prime minister at a press conference before he departs," he told Malaysian media covering the visit.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, will be accompanied by Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI) Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Economy Minister Datuk Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir and senior officials from the Foreign Ministry, MITI and the Economy Ministry.
The trip marks Anwar's first official visit to Turkmenistan and the fifth by a Malaysian prime minister.
He is expected to arrive in Ashgabat at 8.30pm local time on June 18 and attend a dinner with the Malaysian diaspora and friends of Malaysia.
Official events on June 19 will begin with a welcoming ceremony hosted by President Berdimuhamedov at the Presidential Palace, followed by a one-on-one meeting, bilateral talks, the signing of a joint statement by both leaders, and an exchange of agreements and memoranda of understanding already concluded by the two governments.
The prime minister is also scheduled to attend an official luncheon hosted by Berdimuhamedov before performing Friday prayers at the Gypjak Mosque with Turkmenistan's mufti Yalkap Hojagulyyev and members of the local Muslim community.
He will also attend the Turkmenistan-Malaysia Forum, held in conjunction with the 30th anniversary of bilateral cooperation in the oil and gas sector, at the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Turkmenistan, before holding a press conference with Malaysian media and concluding the visit.
Apart from discussions on oil and gas, the visit is expected to explore investment opportunities in transportation, logistics, textiles and agriculture.
Suhaimi said Turkmenistan is also interested in cooperating with Malaysia in halal manufacturing, technology-related industries such as artificial intelligence (AI), and digital animation.
There are currently 177 registered Malaysians in Turkmenistan, most of whom are employees of Petronas Carigali Turkmenistan Sdn Bhd, their family members and staff of other international oil and gas companies.
Bilateral trade totalled RM75.8 million (US$17.71 million) last year, with Malaysia recording a trade surplus of RM75.2 million (US$17.58 million).
Malaysia's main exports were agricultural products, accounting for 68.8 per cent of total exports, particularly coffee, engineering equipment, palm oil and palm oil-based products, and chemical-based products.
With twice-weekly flights between the two countries, Malaysia recorded 10,751 tourist arrivals from Turkmenistan in 2025, up significantly from about 7,545 in 2024 and 1,116 in 2023.
Meanwhile, Turkmenistan Airlines reported a steady flow of around 2,000 Malaysian passengers a month using its services to travel to Jeddah for the umrah pilgrimage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran reveals 14-point draft outlining US deal roadmap
Article:
ISTANBUL: Iran's official news agency IRNA on Wednesday published the full text of a 14‑point memorandum of understanding with the United States.
According to Anadolu Ajansi, the document outlines a framework to end hostilities, launch negotiations toward a final agreement, and address sanctions, maritime security, Iran's nuclear programme and reconstruction.
KEY PROVISIONS INCLUDE:
1. Permanent cessation of military operations on all fronts, including Lebanon, with guarantees of sovereignty.
2. Mutual respect for territorial integrity and non‑interference.
3. Negotiations on a final agreement within 60 days, extendable by consent.
4. Lifting of the US naval blockade within 30 days, with eventual withdrawal of US forces after a final deal.
5. Restoration of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, with Iran consulting Oman and Gulf states.
6. A US$300 billion reconstruction and development programme for Iran with US and regional support.
7. US commitment to end all sanctions under a timetable.
8. Iran's reaffirmation not to produce or acquire nuclear weapons, with talks on civilian nuclear activities.
9. Maintenance of the status quo during negotiations, with no new sanctions or deployments.
10. Immediate US waivers for Iranian oil and petrochemical exports, including banking and transport services.
11. Release of frozen Iranian funds and assets.
12. Establishment of a joint mechanism to monitor compliance.
13. Launch of final agreement talks once key provisions begin implementation.
14. Endorsement of the final agreement through a binding UN Security Council resolution.
US officials also released a draft of the understanding earlier Wednesday. The signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday in Switzerland.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Putrajaya in disrepair: Broken lifts, escalators, and cracked tiles mar city's appeal [WATCH]
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Public squares and facilities here are showing signs of disrepair, with residents and traders raising concerns over maintenance.
This issue gained attention after TikTok user yb_carlosbasri shared a video highlighting the damage.
The video drew a wave of comments from users echoing the same frustrations.
"Even the lift is broken and the escalator doesn't work either. Tourists visit there every day," commented user Ahmad.
Another user, Vindass, called on authorities to act.
"Thank you for raising this, I hope the maintenance team takes the issue seriously and acts swiftly. Well-maintained public facilities reflect our country's image."
A New Straits Times check found cracked floor tiles, broken stairs, and fallen safety railings around the area.
Broken lifts and escalators have also reportedly been out of service for months.
Resident Syahmi Hazim Rezali, 23, a frequent visitor, said the deterioration was hard to miss.
"I've noticed a lot of damage, the broken escalators, for instance. It feels like it's been a long time since I last saw them functioning well," he told the New Straits Times.
He said the lack of maintenance reflects poorly on the country, given the number of tourists who visit the area.
"A lot of tourists come here, with broken facilities, it's quite bad for our image as Malaysians.
"We have government bodies and policies meant to maintain these things; when they stay broken for this long, people start to question their authority and efficiency," he said.
An elderly visitor, who declined to be interviewed, was overheard saying the lifts had been out of order for some time and that maintenance appeared to have slipped compared to previous years.
A nearby shopkeeper, who asked not to be named, said the lifts and escalators near his premises had been broken for at least three months.
"The repair process is very slow, it definitely affects business because people are less inclined to come here now.
"Compared to before, the crowd has thinned out significantly," he said.
NST has reached out to Putrajaya Corporation for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit opens easier against US dollar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:The ringgit opened lower against the US dollar on Thursday as sentiment turned cautious after the US Federal Reserve adopted a hawkish tone, driving US Treasury yields higher.
At 8 am, the local currency weakened to 4.0730/0820 against the greenback from 4.0665/0700 at Tuesday's close.
The market was closed on Wednesday for the Awal Muharram public holiday.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.85 per cent to 100.382 points after the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) signalled a more hawkish monetary policy outlook.
"It appears the Fed is likely to raise the federal funds rate by 25 basis points this year, based on its latest quarterly projections, while the FOMC's statement underscored that price stability remains its primary focus.
"Two-year US Treasury yields rose 13 basis points to 4.18 per cent. Against this backdrop, the ringgit is expected to remain under pressure today as the US dollar is likely to stay well supported by the Fed's latest decision," he told Bernama.
Meanwhile, Mohd Afzanizam said reports of an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to end the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz had weighed on crude oil prices.
"WTI and Brent crude are currently trading at US$76.06 and US$79.55 per barrel, respectively. While lower oil prices could provide some support to broader market sentiment, the Fed's hawkish stance is likely to remain the dominant market driver," he added.
At the opening, the ringgit was mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
It was almost unchanged against the Japanese yen at 2.5360/5417 from 2.5360/5384 at Tuesday's close, strengthened against the British pound to 5.4175/4295 from 5.4552/4599, and appreciated versus the euro to 4.6868/6972 from 4.7175/7216.
Against regional currencies, the local note was mixed.
It rose against the Thai baht to 12.4518/4870 from 12.5027/5192 at Tuesday's close and strengthened against the Singapore dollar to 3.1623/1695 from 3.1722/1752.
However, it was little changed against the Indonesian rupiah at 229.3/229.9 from 229.3/229.7 and the Philippine peso at 6.74/6.76 from 6.74/6.75.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar, Putin discuss energy security, trade in local currencies
Article:
KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Russian President Vladimir Putin discussed strengthening bilateral cooperation, focusing on energy security and expanding trade.
In a statement, the Prime Minister's Office said Anwar and Putin exchanged views on long‑term energy collaboration and oil supplies, alongside opportunities in new technologies, cybersecurity, digitalisation, artificial intelligence (AI), modern agriculture and pharmaceuticals.
The talks also covered visa liberalisation and mechanisms to expand trade and investment using local currencies, namely the ringgit and the ruble.
"The Madani government will continue to take strategic and proactive measures to strengthen energy security, including through international cooperation that ensures stability, continuity and resilience of supplies for national prosperity," the statement said.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, is in Kazan, the capital of Tatarstan, for the Asean‑Russia Commemorative Summit from June 17 to 18.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jalan Semabok-Ayer Keroh shut due to sinkhole
Article:
Your browser does not support the audio element.
KUALA LUMPUR: A 20m stretch of Jalan Semabok-Ayer Keroh here has been closed to traffic after a burst water pipe formed a sinkhole yesterday.
The Melaka Tengah Public Works Department said the incident occurred at 9am near the traffic light at Masjid Bukit Bayan, along Section 3.4 of Federal Route 264.
It said the sinkhole was caused by a burst pipe belonging to Syarikat Air Melaka Bhd, adding that the weather was fair at the time of the incident.
"As an initial measure, we have installed warning and diversion signs at the site.
"Motorists are advised to use the alternative route via Jalan Demang Husin along FT264 while the road is closed."
The update was provided by the department and Selia Selenggara Selatan Sdn Bhd, the road maintenance concessionaire.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: I have no choice, says mum who delivers food with special child in tow
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Strapping her daughter to her chest, Azimah Abd Rahim weaves through traffic delivering food.
She has tried leaving her daughter with babysitters, but every attempt ended in mistreatment.
The experience left her traumatised. As a result, she takes her daughter with her wherever she works, despite stares and cruel remarks from strangers.
"I often get scolded by customers. They ask, 'What's wrong with you? Don't you love your child?' I just ignore them. Some even say, 'If you don't love your child, you might as well die.'
"It's not that I don't want to send her to a babysitter. Every evening when I came home, I saw bruises from her neck down to her feet. When I asked what happened, the babysitter said she had fallen.
"Later, during a hospital appointment, the doctor told me, 'These aren't fall injuries. These are pinch marks.' Ever since then, I've taken care of her myself," she said.
Azimah's daughter, Nur Ainul Zahirah Rahidi, 8, is blind, autistic and has G6PD deficiency, a genetic condition that requires careful medical management.
A video of Azimah cradling her daughter after a food delivery went viral a few years ago.
The clip touched many Malaysians. Behind it, however, lies a story of resilience, heartbreak and a mother's unwavering determination to rebuild her life after a divorce.
"After we divorced, I had no money and no job. My ex-husband did give me a monthly allowance, but because of his poor health, it was very little and barely covered anything.
"My expenses aren't limited to food and shelter. I also need money for her schooling, therapy and hospital visits."
Azimah turned to food delivery work during the Covid-19 Movement Control Order after delays in welfare assistance.
"At that time, funds from the Social Welfare Department did not come in for months. Diapers, milk, everything was running out. I was desperate. I even borrowed money to register as a rider."
She said the work was exhausting and risky, but it allowed her to put food on the table.
Azimah now earns between RM500 and RM700 a month and receives RM600 in zakat assistance. Most of the money goes towards rent, hospital visits, therapy and other essential expenses.
Azimah, who has been diagnosed with depression, is also managing her own health challenges while struggling to make ends meet.
Azimah said her welfare aid was discontinued after she received zakat assistance.
The NST contacted the Social Welfare Department for clarification on the types of assistance available.
The department said that recipients of zakat assistance could still apply for government aid.
However, it said zakat assistance was taken into account as part of an applicant's total household income when assessing eligibility for assistance.
"For example, Salmah is a single mother raising five school-going children. She earns RM600 a month by babysitting her neighbours' children.
"She also receives RM500 a month in zakat assistance. Despite this, Salmah remains eligible for Children Assistance Scheme because her total household income is below the hardcore poverty threshold of RM1,198," the department said.
Azimah said she believed there were others facing greater hardship and she did not want to beg for help.
However, she hopes the government will review its aid policies and provide targeted support for single mothers raising children with disabilities.
She also urged the government to provide housing assistance for single mothers, particularly those caring for children with disabilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Historic breakthrough as UM reaches 56th in QS rankings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya (UM) has achieved its highest‑ever position in the QS World University Rankings, rising from 58th to 56th.
The achievement reinforces UM's status as Malaysia's top‑ranked university and places it among the top 3.7 per cent of institutions worldwide.
Among Malaysian universities, UM ranked first in Academic Reputation, Employer Reputation, Employment Outcomes and Sustainability.
Globally, it placed 56th, 61st, 184th and 166th respectively in these indicators. UM also secured strong positions in International Research Network (125th) and International Students (153rd).
Vice‑Chancellor Datuk Seri Professor  Dr Noor Azuan Abu Osman described the achievement as a landmark moment of institutional pride and national significance.
"It is the outcome of a university that has chosen discipline over complacency, execution over rhetoric, and collective purpose over conventional normalcy," he said.
He added the success reflected the strength of a mission‑led institution powered by academics, researchers, students, staff and leadership teams.
"It affirms that when strategic vision is matched with clarity, cohesion, courage and sustained action, Universiti Malaya will not merely participate in the global higher education landscape, we are there to define it," he said.
Noor Azuan credited clear planning, institutional alignment, teamwork and focus on critical sectors. He highlighted UM's efforts in frontier research, digital transformation, sustainability, talent excellence, innovation, industry relevance and global partnerships.
He stressed that rankings matter as they reflect quality, trust and impact, but UM's broader mission is to spearhead national growth, resilience and global knowledge.
UM's overall score for the 2027 edition stands at 81.2 across QS performance lenses of Research and Discovery, Employability and Outcomes, Sustainability, Learning Experience and Global Engagement.
It recorded 92.8 for Academic Reputation and 93.1 for Employer Reputation, while scores of 93.4 for International Research Network, 93.1 for International Students and 83.7 for International Faculty reflect its growing international profile.
The QS World University Rankings 2027 evaluated 8,808 institutions worldwide, including 32 from Malaysia. Only 1,504 institutions were published across 106 locations.
UM, celebrating its 121st anniversary in 2026, has produced numerous leaders and scholars who have made national and global impact.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Russia, Malaysia well placed to deepen ties, says Putin
Article:
KAZAN: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia and Malaysia have built a strong foundation for expanding cooperation as both countries approach the 60th anniversary of diplomatic relations next year.
Speaking during a meeting with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on the sidelines of the Asean‑Russia Commemorative Summit, Putin said the milestone comes with "substantive positive experience" in bilateral engagement.
"Next year will mark the 60th anniversary of diplomatic relations between our countries. We are approaching this date with substantive positive experience of cooperation and serious coordination towards further expansion," he said.
Putin noted that Russia and Malaysia maintain regular contacts through ministries, agencies and parliaments, while an intergovernmental commission oversees economic, scientific, technical and cultural projects.
He said bilateral trade grew 12.9 per cent in 2025, adding that Russia attaches importance to cooperation in education, science, technology, tourism and humanitarian ties.
Putin welcomed Malaysia's support for strengthening the Russia‑Asean strategic partnership, recalling that the first summit‑level meeting was held in Malaysia in 2005.
He described Malaysia as a long‑standing partner, with ties consistently based on mutual respect and consideration of interests.
Putin also recalled previous engagements with Malaysian leaders and conveyed best wishes to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia.
"I kindly ask you to send my best wishes to him," he said.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Overweight' call kept on consumer sector
Article:
Your browser does not support the audio element.
KUALA LUMPUR: The consumer products sector is expected to remain defensive amid market volatility, underpinned by resilient domestic consumption and earnings visibility, according to RHB Research.
In a note, the firm said fiscal support from the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme, alongside fuel subsidies, should help sustain household spending and contain inflationary pressures.
A prolonged Middle East conflict could dampen sentiment and escalate cost pressures.
However, RHB noted the year-on-year impact may be muted by the low base of the second and third quarters of 2025, a period heavily depressed by uncertainties surrounding United States' reciprocal tariffs.
Supportive factors such as a stronger ringgit, stabilising commodity prices and the Visit Malaysia Year 2026 campaign are also expected to help cushion downside risks.
RHB Research noted that the sector continues to benefit from steady sales trends driven by a healthy labour market and operating leverage, with the first quarter of 2026 results broadly in line with expectations as festive demand from the Lunar New Year and Aidilfitri boosted earnings.
The firm said that of 17 stocks under coverage, nine met expectations, seven missed them and one exceeded forecasts.
However, management guidance has turned more cautious due to geopolitical uncertainties and potential inflationary pressures, particularly on raw materials, logistics and utilities, prompting companies to explore cost mitigation and demand-stimulation measures.
Despite this, the firm said supply chain disruptions remain limited due to diversified sourcing and adequate inventory buffers.
It said that the sector's valuation has also become more attractive after derating to about 23 times forward price-to-earnings, close to 1.5 standard deviations below its long-term mean.
RHB Research has maintained an "overweight" stance on the sector, with top picks, including Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd, Farm Fresh Bhd, MR DIY Group (M) Bhd, and Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd.
The firm cited their defensive characteristics, pricing power and exposure to value-seeking consumption trends.
It highlighted QL Resources Bhd following a recent recommendation change, noting its valuation has become more attractive ahead of an expected recovery in earnings growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar commends Putin's stance on Gaza and Iran
Article:
KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation to Russian President Vladimir Putin for Russia's consistent position on Gaza and the geopolitical situation involving Iran.
During their meeting today, Anwar said Russia had maintained a principled stance on both issues.
"I must take this opportunity to thank you for your position vis‑à‑vis Gaza and now Iran. You have taken a very consistent, principled position," he said in his opening remarks.
Anwar added that Malaysians and many others admired Putin's steadfastness and conviction.
Geopolitically, Gaza and Iran remain key Middle East issues attracting the attention of major powers, including the United States, Russia and China.
Russia has consistently supported a ceasefire in Gaza, humanitarian access for Palestinians and diplomatic solutions to regional conflicts. Moscow has also maintained close ties with Teheran while advocating dialogue and negotiated settlements over military confrontation.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Free-float ringgit regime seen as effective amid volatility, say economists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's free-floating ringgit regime, supported by Bank Negara Malaysia's reliance on non-interest rate interventions, is viewed by economists as effective in managing currency volatility.
The central bank's policy framework, together with liquidity management measures, has helped anchor the ringgit around the RM4.00 level this year, despite periods of volatility. The currency's recent 3.0–4.0 per cent weakening against the US dollar over the past week has, however, renewed focus on its near-term trajectory.
Economist Dr Geoffrey Williams said the latest fluctuations largely reflect external factors, particularly widening US–Malaysia interest rate differentials.
The US Federal Reserve's aggressive tightening cycle, which lifted rates from near zero to above 5.0 per cent, has contrasted with Bank Negara's more measured approach, where the overnight policy rate has been held at 3.0 per cent to support domestic growth and financial stability.
The ringgit has performed strongly since the start of the year, appreciating from around RM4.05 against the US dollar in January to trade within the RM3.90–RM4.07 range by mid-year, its strongest levels since 2018.
On Tuesday, however, the currency closed lower against the US dollar as investors stayed cautious following the Bank of Japan's decision to raise interest rates. At 6pm, the ringgit eased to 4.0665/0700 versus the greenback, compared with 4.0485/0525 at Monday's close.
"The recent weakening of the ringgit is really part of normal movements in exchange rates due to news and short-term events. It is not obvious that the strength of the ringgit is losing momentum," he told Business Times.
He said exchange rates are ultimately driven by supply and demand in the foreign exchange market, adding that labels such as "overvalued" or "undervalued" are often of limited analytical value.
Over the longer term, the currency has moved through three clear regimes – post-peg stability, a multi-year depreciation phase, and more recently a policy-supported stabilisation with intermittent appreciation. The key question is whether this stabilisation can be sustained.
Malaysia ended its RM3.80 per US dollar peg in July 2005, moving to a managed float. The ringgit then strengthened steadily, peaking above RM3.03 by early 2011, supported by strong commodity prices, robust trade surpluses, sustained foreign direct investment inflows and ample foreign reserves, placing it among the region's stronger currencies.
That trend reversed after 2014, as collapsing global crude oil prices eroded energy revenues and domestic political uncertainty linked to the 1MDB scandal weighed on investor sentiment. The combined shock triggered capital outflows, pushing the ringgit below RM4.35 by 2016.
The post-pandemic period brought renewed volatility. From around RM4.22 in 2022, the currency weakened to a trough near RM4.79 in early 2024 before recovering to about RM4.06 currently.
Dr Williams said the current cycle is fundamentally different from previous boom-and-bust episodes, being underpinned by stronger macroeconomic fundamentals and policy discipline.
"The current cycle is not one of boom and bust; it is based on strong fundamentals and conservative policies. So long as this continues the risks of a sharp correction are low. If this is disturbed, the risks are higher," he said.
He added that Bank Negara's framework, combining a free-floating exchange rate with non-interest rate interventions, has been effective in maintaining stability.
This has been reinforced by coordinated efforts with government-linked companies and government-linked investment companies to repatriate and convert overseas earnings into ringgit liquidity, helping support FX demand.
"The repatriation policy is not just encouragement by Bank Negara; there have been minor changes in regulations too. So this process will continue," he said.
However, Dr Williams cautioned that external support from high commodity prices, particularly oil and palm oil, could moderate if global prices ease, potentially reducing one of the ringgit's key tailwinds.
He also pointed to fiscal pressures, noting that subsidy costs have eased from about RM7.5 billion per month in April to roughly half that level but remain a key structural consideration.
"The real risk is political instability and the upcoming elections," he said.
On the outlook for policy, Dr Williams said the current framework should be maintained.
"I think the ringgit will remain above RM4.00 even if the Federal Reserve cuts rates," he said, adding that political continuity remains critical to sustaining investor confidence.
"The current recovery cycle is vulnerable to policy changes; if the government changes, this is why political stability is essential at the moment. If there is a general election, the best outcome would be a strong coalition with the current policies and leadership," Dr Williams said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Economists see room for Brent crude to ease toward US$70 as supply stability supports downside risk
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Brent crude may have further downside risk, with economists saying prices could ease toward pre-conflict levels of around US$70 per barrel if global supply routes remain stable and no new disruptions occur.
Oil prices have already retreated to a three-month low, reflecting easing risk premiums in the market. At the time of writing, Brent crude futures fell to US$83.68 per barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) stood at US$80.75 per barrel.
Both benchmarks declined to their lowest levels since March 10 on Monday, after already sliding more than three per cent on Friday.
Universiti Teknologi Mara Business Management Faculty senior lecturer Dr Mohamad Idham Md Razak told Business Times that the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz will strengthen supply expectations and ease the geopolitical risk premium that had previously supported higher oil prices.
"However, the pace of adjustment will likely be gradual, as global oil pricing also depends on broader factors such as OPEC+ production decisions, demand trends from major economies, and seasonal consumption patterns," he said.
Universiti Teknologi Malaysia associate professor in property economics Dr Muhammad Najib Razali said a return of oil prices to around US$70 per barrel would require more than just a political announcement.
"It would require full and credible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, normalisation of tanker movement, lower war-risk insurance, no renewed military incidents, and confidence that supply disruptions will not recur," he told Business Times.
Najib said that for Malaysia, a move in Brent crude towards US$70 per barrel would be disinflationary.
He said sectors such as transport, logistics, aviation, manufacturing, construction, agriculture, retail, and consumers are broadly benefiting from lower fuel and distribution costs.
"Airlines would benefit from lower jet fuel costs, logistics firms from lower diesel and freight-related costs, and manufacturers from lower energy and transport expenses," he said.
However, Najib said a sharp decline in Brent crude would have a negative income effect on the upstream oil and gas segment.
He added that oil producers, oilfield service providers, and petroleum-linked contractors could face weaker cash flows, lower capital expenditure, and reduced demand for services.
"The fiscal effect is also mixed: lower oil prices reduce subsidy pressure, but they may also reduce petroleum-related revenue, tax receipts, and dividends," he said.
Najib said that while Brent crude could move towards US$70 per barrel under a benign scenario, such an outcome is not guaranteed.
"The more likely near-term path is a gradual easing with volatility, because markets will wait for evidence that the Strait of Hormuz reopening is durable and that shipping, insurance, and supply-chain risks have normalised," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Farm Fresh targets Cambodia dairy market with 3Q plant launch
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Farm Fresh Bhd is accelerating its regional expansion with a new dairy processing plant in Cambodia expected to commence full operations in the third quarter of this year, positioning the company to capitalise on growing demand for locally supplied milk products in a market historically dominated by imports.
The plant is being developed with a local partner. Once operational, it will house four production lines with an annual capacity of 20 million litres of pasteurised milk and 40 million litres of ultra-high temperature (UHT) milk, said Farm Fresh group chief financial officer, Mohd Khairul Mat Hassan.
Farm Fresh began laying the groundwork for its Cambodia expansion in mid-2025, following geopolitical tensions between Cambodia and Thailand that disrupted established supply chains and created opportunities for new market entrants.
Prior to that, Cambodia's dairy market was largely supplied by two major players. However, growing consumer preference for non-Thai products has opened up significant opportunities for alternative suppliers, particularly in the pasteurised milk segment, which is estimated to be worth more than RM200 million annually.
[[nid:1466036]]
Khairul told Business Times that the UHT milk market is believed to be three to four times larger, making Cambodia one of the company's most promising overseas growth markets.
He said the company's immediate priority is to rapidly scale up its chilled milk business in Cambodia.
He said that while Farm Fresh has been exporting products into the country, transportation costs and delivery lead times have constrained growth.
Khairul said the new plant is expected to significantly improve logistics efficiency and strengthen its competitive position.
He added that raw milk ingredients for the Cambodian operations will be sourced primarily from Australia, where Farm Fresh operates a dairy processing facility capable of handling 84 million litres annually.
Despite its overseas ambitions, Malaysia remains Farm Fresh's core market, accounting for about 80 per cent of total revenue. Overseas revenue is derived from Singapore, Australia, and the Philippines.
Khairul said the Philippines and Cambodia are expected to become increasingly important contributors, although neither market is expected to account for more than 10 per cent of total revenue individually.
He said its Philippines operations are gaining traction, while domestic expansion continues through deeper market penetration in Sabah and Sarawak.
Separately, Farm Fresh's new integrated processing facility in Bandar Baru Enstek, Negeri Sembilan, is expected to commence production in the second half of this year. The plant will serve as the company's central processing hub and significantly expand its manufacturing capabilities.
Once fully operational, the Enstek facility will increase Farm Fresh's ice cream production capacity to one million pieces per day from the current combined capacity of 450,000 pieces daily across its Taiping and Bandar Sri Damansara plants.
Looking ahead, Khairul said Farm Fresh remains optimistic about sustaining growth through a combination of product innovation, category expansion and deeper regional market penetration across Southeast Asia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar thanks Putin for backing energy cooperation with Petronas
Article:
KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation to Russian President Vladimir Putin for Russia's support in strengthening energy cooperation with Malaysia, particularly Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas).
Anwar said bilateral mechanisms between Malaysia and Russia have expanded across trade, investment, the halal economy and finance. He added that Malaysia is close to finalising visa liberalisation arrangements with Russia, which would further facilitate exchanges and cooperation.
Russia aims to abolish visa requirements for citizens of Malaysia, Indonesia and Kuwait this year, with the Russian Foreign Ministry stepping up efforts to finalise the agreements.
Anwar thanked Putin for Russia's support, noting that Petronas had engaged with Rosneft and other companies to advance collaboration.
"You have given fantastic support. Thank you very much. Petronas was here with your counterpart Rosneft and also met other companies," he said.
PJSC Rosneft Oil Company, Russia's largest integrated energy corporation, accounts for about 40 per cent of the country's oil production.
In April, Anwar said Petronas would negotiate with Russia to secure sufficient oil supplies for Malaysia, with good bilateral ties placing the country in a favourable position.
The Prime Minister also expressed hope for improved air connectivity between Malaysia and Russian cities, including Moscow and Kazan, to boost tourism, trade and people‑to‑people links.
He said both countries are exploring cooperation in the halal economy and finance, while trade and investment discussions continue to progress.
In 2024, Asean‑Russia trade totalled US$18.1 billion, with Russian FDI in Asean recorded at RM367.90 million (US$92.97 million). For Malaysia, Russia was its ninth‑largest European trading partner in 2025, with trade valued at RM8.72 billion (US$2.04 billion).
Malaysia's main exports to Russia include electrical and electronic products, machinery, equipment and processed food, while imports comprise petroleum products, minerals and chemicals.
Anwar is in Kazan, the capital of Tatarstan, for the Asean‑Russia Commemorative Summit from June 17 to 18.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd
